prompt: Three-quarter exterior view of an old rotted pressure-treated wood deck behind a 1990s Haverhill split-level home, BEFORE rebuild. Gray-faded decking boards with visible cupping and a few obviously cracked planks, a soft and bouncy spot near the sliding door. Rusty galvanized fasteners standing proud above the wood surface. Loose 2x2 spindle railing with a couple missing balusters. Patchy lawn beyond. Shot with a 35mm prime lens at a low three-quarter angle, overcast natural daylight emphasises the silver-gray weathering on every plank, flat shadows. A pair of red work gloves and a pry bar sit on the deck as the only fully saturated color, demolition starting. Slightly desaturated palette, NOT HGTV bright, NOT real-estate-photographer over-lit. Working-contractor proof-of-work feel. No readable text, no logos.
